summary: Typosquat of @solana-labs/spl-token executes install-time host reconnaissance and exfiltration
details: Package name @solana-labs/spl-toke is a single-character-deletion typosquat of the official @solana-labs/spl-token package. package.json declares `"postinstall": "node install.js"`, which fires automatically on `npm install`. install.js reads host identifiers (hostname references at lines 10, 35, 37), shells out via curl (line 116), and performs an HTTP POST to an external endpoint (line 56). This is the classic install-time host-reconnaissance + exfiltration pattern: harvest installer machine identity and ship it to an attacker-controlled destination, with no benign purpose for the package's advertised function (Solana SPL token library). The combination of (a) typosquat of a widely-used Solana ecosystem package, (b) auto-executing postinstall hook, and (c) curl + POST + hostname read in the install script is unambiguous supply-chain attack shape.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
